questions.question: Which ancient university was located in Taxila, one of the earliest centers of higher learning in India?
questions.explanation: Takshashila (Taxila) was an ancient university renowned for higher education in ancient India, especially during the Mauryan and Gupta periods.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in India after his victory at Panipat in 1526.

questions.question: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920 as a mass protest against British rule.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Salt Satyagraha in 1930?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Salt Satyagraha as part of his civil disobedience movement against salt taxes.

questions.question: Which event marked the end of the Indian freedom struggle and India's independence?
questions.explanation: India gained independence on August 15, 1947, marking the end of British colonial rule.

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar' due to frequent flooding?
questions.explanation: The Kosi River is called the 'Sorrow of Bihar' because of recurrent floods affecting the region.

questions.question: The Indian Desert is mainly found in which state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert, also known as the Indian Desert, is primarily located in Rajasthan.

questions.question: Which is the largest freshwater lake in India?
questions.explanation: Vembanad Lake in Kerala is the largest freshwater lake in India.

questions.question: Which Indian state is the leading producer of tea?
questions.explanation: Assam is the largest producer of tea in India, known worldwide for its Assam tea.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India (as of 2026-05-13)?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu is the current President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: The Lok Sabha is composed of members directly elected by whom?
questions.explanation: Members of the Lok Sabha are directly elected by Indian citizens aged 18 and above through general elections.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution guarantees Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 12-35 of the Indian Constitution collectively guarantee Fundamental Rights to citizens.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Justice of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Justice of India.

questions.question: The Panchayati Raj system was constitutionalized in which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992, constitutionalized the Panchayati Raj system.

questions.question: The Budget of the Government of India is presented by whom?
questions.explanation: The Finance Minister presents the Union Budget in Parliament.

questions.question: Which organization is responsible for controlling inflation and monetary policy in India?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) controls monetary policy and inflation in India.

questions.question: GDP stands for?
questions.explanation: GDP stands for Gross Domestic Product, measuring the total value of goods and services produced in a country.

questions.question: Which Five Year Plan emphasized 'Self-Reliance'?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Five Year Plan (1974-1979) emphasized 'Self-Reliance'.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the 'Red Planet' due to its reddish appearance caused by iron oxide on its surface.

questions.question: The process of photosynthesis occurs in which part of a plant?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis primarily occurs in the leaves of plants, where chlorophyll captures sunlight.

questions.question: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere, making it the most abundant gas.

questions.question: The Hubble Space Telescope is primarily used to observe?
questions.explanation: Hubble Space Telescope observes deep space, galaxies, stars, and cosmic phenomena beyond Earth's atmosphere.

questions.question: Which of the following is a biodiversity hotspot in India?
questions.explanation: Western Ghats is a recognized biodiversity hotspot in India due to its rich endemic species and ecological significance.

questions.question: The Convention on Biological Diversity was adopted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Convention on Biological Diversity was adopted in 1992 during the Earth Summit in Rio de Janeiro.

questions.question: Which international agreement aims to combat climate change?
questions.explanation: The Paris Agreement is a global treaty to limit global warming and combat climate change.

questions.question: Which Indian law regulates environmental protection?
questions.explanation: The Environment Protection Act, 1986, is a comprehensive law for environmental regulation in India.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Minister of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi has been the Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar has been serving as the External Affairs Minister since 2019.

questions.question: Which prestigious award was recently conferred upon Indian athlete Neeraj Chopra?
questions.explanation: Neeraj Chopra received the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna, India's highest sporting honor.

questions.question: The 'Startup India' scheme was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: Startup India was launched in 2016 to promote entrepreneurship and innovation.
